Also offers gynecological services, mental health care, physical therapy, diabetes education classes, prescription medicine discounts, assistance in applying for OHP, referrals to outside providers, and information about other social services. 3550 SW Borland Road Tualatin, OR 97062 Eligibility: Individuals who are at or below 400% of the Federal Poverty Line (FPL) Hours: Monday/Thursday 3pm-7pm, Wednesday 9am-1pm Email (503) 974-8887 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CASCADE AIDS PROJECT PRISM HEALTH Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides primary health care and behavioral health. Offers laboratory and pharmacy services. 2236 SE Belmont Street Portland, OR 97214 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday/Tuesday/Wednesday/Friday 8:30am-5pm, Thursday 9am-5pm Email (503) 445-7699 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CENTRAL CITY CONCERN OLD TOWN CLINIC Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Old Town Clinic offers a patient-centered primary care. Services include: care for acute and chronic illnesses, women's health services, such as PAPS, pelvic exams, and breast exams, behavioral healthcare evaluation, substance abuse assessment, naturopathic and acupuncture services. Also offers a variety of wellness programs and activities available to patients, such as yoga. Urgent care for established patients. The pharmacy provides medication education and therapy management, as well as anticoagulation therapy management to current clients. 726 W Burnside Street Portland, OR 97209 Eligibility: Age 18 and older Hours: Clinic hours: Monday/Tuesday/Thursday/Friday 8am-5pm, Wednesday 9am-5pm Pharmacy hours: Monday/Tuesday/Thursday/Friday 8:30am-5:30, Wednesday 9:30am-5:30pm Email (503) 228-4533 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CHILDREN'S COMMUNITY CLINIC Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides primary pediatric care. Services include well baby and child checkups, physical exams, acute care (flu, infection, wound care), chronic care (asthma, allergies, diabetes), pelvic and Pap exams, contraception, sexually transmitted disease screening and treatment, and immunizations. 2252 Lloyd Center Portland, OR 97232 Eligibility: Ages 0-21 Hours: Primary care: Monday-Friday 9am-noon and 1pm-5pm Email (503) 284-5239 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CLACKAMAS FREE CLINIC VOLUNTEERS IN MEDICINE CLACKAMAS FREE CLINIC Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides primary medical services for individuals without access to health care. Volunteer medical professionals offer routine preventative and non-emergency medical care. For chronic conditions such as hypertension, diabetes, asthma, and heart disease, care is provided in addition to education and equipment for self-care. Eye exams and glasses are also available. More specialized services that cannot be provided on-site are referred to a network of specialty physicians in the region. 19600 Molalla Avenue Clairmont Hall Oregon City, OR 97045 Eligibility: -Age 18 and older with income -400% of the federal poverty guidelines for the previous 3 months -No health insurance or access to health care. -Gap insurance/medical care needed temporarily, underinsured
